aou_req__bitmanipulation__concurrent_acc.content:
The user shall implement external synchronization mechanisms (e.g., mutexes, atomic operations, or locks) when accessing or modifying the same integral value from multiple threads concurrently.

Note: The library provides no internal thread safety guarantees.
aou_req__bitmanipulation__concurrent_acc.title: External Synchronization Required for Concurrent Access

aou_req__bitmanipulation__enum_constrain.content: The user shall use scoped enumeration types (enum class) whose enumerators are defined as non-zero power-of-two values.
aou_req__bitmanipulation__enum_constrain.title: Bitmask Enum Value Constraints

aou_req__bitmanipulation__type_constrain.content:
The user shall use bit manipulation functions only with integral types (integers, enumerations) as specified in the library's type constraints.

Note: Operations on floating-point or non-integral types are not supported.
aou_req__bitmanipulation__type_constrain.title: Integral Type Constraints

aou_req__concurrency__coop_cancellation.content: Users shall implement cancellation logic in long-running tasks to respond to stop_token signals.
aou_req__concurrency__coop_cancellation.title: Cooperative Cancellation Handling

aou_req__concurrency__memory_management.content: Users shall handle task submission failures when the bounded memory resource is exhausted and implement appropriate backpressure mechanisms.
aou_req__concurrency__memory_management.title: Memory Management on Task Submission Failure

aou_req__concurrency__thread_pool_sizing.content: Users shall size ThreadPool according to task characteristics and avoid submitting blocking tasks that will starve the thread pool.
aou_req__concurrency__thread_pool_sizing.title: Thread Pool Sizing

aou_req__concurrency__thread_safety.content: Users shall implement appropriate synchronization mechanisms when sharing state between tasks, as the library only guarantees thread-safe task submission and execution scheduling.
aou_req__concurrency__thread_safety.title: Thread Safety

aou_req__containers__bounds_checking.content: The user shall ensure that all index-based access operations use valid indices within the container's current size range to prevent out-of-bounds access and undefined behavior.
aou_req__containers__bounds_checking.title: Index Bounds Checking

aou_req__containers__capacity_management.content: The user shall verify sufficient capacity or handle allocation failures before insertion operations to prevent exceeding container limits and undefined behavior.
aou_req__containers__capacity_management.title: Check Capacity

aou_req__containers__ele_type_requiremen.content: The user shall ensure that element types are copy constructible, move constructible and destructible, and additionally for intrusive containers that they provide the required intrusive node members.
aou_req__containers__ele_type_requiremen.title: Element Type Requirements

aou_req__containers__element_lifetime.content: The user shall ensure that elements stored in containers remain valid throughout their lifetime in the container, and for intrusive containers, that element objects are not destroyed or moved while contained within the container.
aou_req__containers__element_lifetime.title: Element Lifetime and Ownership

aou_req__containers__iterator_validity.content: The user shall ensure that iterators are not used after operations that invalidate them and refresh iterators after modifying operations.
aou_req__containers__iterator_validity.title: Iterator Validity

aou_req__containers__memory_management.content: The user shall provide sufficient memory resources for all container operations.
aou_req__containers__memory_management.title: Memory Resource Management

aou_req__containers__state_verification.content: The user shall verify container state before performing operations that depend on specific states, such as checking if a container is non-empty before accessing elements.
aou_req__containers__state_verification.title: Container State Verification

aou_req__containers__thread_safety.content: The user shall implement external synchronization mechanisms when accessing or modifying container objects from multiple threads concurrently, as the library provides no internal thread safety guarantees.
aou_req__containers__thread_safety.title: Thread Safety

aou_req__filesystem__error_handling.content: Users shall handle all function return values and take appropriate action on errors.
aou_req__filesystem__error_handling.title: Error Handling

aou_req__filesystem__low_level_use_only.content: Users shall use this library only when they need to manipulate files, directories, and paths at the operating system level beyond what higher-level storage abstractions provide, accepting reduced portability to other platforms.
aou_req__filesystem__low_level_use_only.title: Low-Level Use Only

aou_req__filesystem__path_validation.content:
User shall ensure that all paths passed to the filesystem library are valid and originate from trusted sources.

Note: The library does not perform any path sanitization or validation.
aou_req__filesystem__path_validation.title: Path Validation

aou_req__filesystem__performance.content:
Users shall not assume any performance characteristics.

Note: Filesystem operations are subject to underlying OS and storage performance.
aou_req__filesystem__performance.title: Performance

aou_req__filesystem__supported_path_form.content: Users shall not use Windows paths with this library.
aou_req__filesystem__supported_path_form.title: Supported Path Formats

aou_req__filesystem__thread_safety.content: Users shall implement appropriate synchronization mechanisms when accessing shared filesystem resources concurrently.
aou_req__filesystem__thread_safety.title: Thread Safety

aou_req__flatbuffers__access_control.content:
The user shall ensure access control and manipulation prevention on the FlatBuffers binary files.

.. note::
   This can be done by the hosting process and system configuration (e.g. by using dm-verity).
aou_req__flatbuffers__access_control.title: FlatBuffers access control

aou_req__flatbuffers__data_integrity.content:
The user shall provide FlatBuffers binary data as input which is not corrupted due to HW, QM SW, or communication channel errors.

.. note::
   The FlatBuffers-Library verification mechanism only validates structural well-formedness,
   not payload data integrity. Data integrity shall be ensured by external means such as a safe
   read-only filesystem for FlatBuffers binary file storage or a checksum protection on the
   FlatBuffers binary file content.
aou_req__flatbuffers__data_integrity.title: FlatBuffers data integrity

aou_req__flatbuffers__verify_structure.content:
The user shall apply the verification mechanism provided by :need:`comp_req__flatbuffers__verification`
to a loaded buffer and confirm a successful result before accessing any data from that buffer.

.. note::
   Accessing data from an unverified or malformed buffer results in undefined behaviour.
   The verification mechanism only checks structural well-formedness; it does not replace
   the data integrity measures required by :need:`aou_req__flatbuffers__data_integrity`.
aou_req__flatbuffers__verify_structure.title: FlatBuffers buffer verification before access

aou_req__flatbuffers__verify_version.content:
The user shall apply the version check mechanism provided by :need:`comp_req__flatbuffers__version_check`
to a loaded buffer and confirm a successful result before accessing any data from that buffer.

.. note::
   This provides early detection of schema or version mismatches before any further buffer data is
   verified or accessed.
aou_req__flatbuffers__verify_version.title: FlatBuffers buffer version check before access

aou_req__json__access_control.content:
The user shall ensure access control and manipulation prevention on the JSON files.

Note: This can be done by the hosting process and system configuration (e.g. by using dm-verity).
aou_req__json__access_control.title: Access control

aou_req__json__data_integrity.content:
The user shall provide a string as input which is not corrupted due to HW or QM SW errors.

Note: This could be achieved by using a safe read-only filesystem for JSON file storage or a checksum protection on the JSON file content.
aou_req__json__data_integrity.title: JSON data integrity

aou_req__result__error_domain_implementa.content: The user shall implement error domain classes and error code enumerations according to the library's interface specification.
aou_req__result__error_domain_implementa.title: Error Domain Implementation

aou_req__result__error_reaction.content: The user shall implement error reaction mechanisms that transition the system to a safe state when error conditions are detected in Result objects.
aou_req__result__error_reaction.title: Error Reaction and Safe State

aou_req__result__resource_lifetime.content: The user shall ensure that error domain objects and referenced resources remain valid throughout the entire lifetime of any dependent Result or Error objects.
aou_req__result__resource_lifetime.title: Resource Lifetime

aou_req__result__thread_safety.content: The user shall implement external synchronization mechanisms (e.g., mutexes, atomic operations, or locks) when accessing or modifying Result objects from multiple threads concurrently, as the library provides no internal thread safety guarantees.
aou_req__result__thread_safety.title: Thread Safety

aou_req__result__value_handling.content: The user shall check and handle both successful and error states of Result objects before accessing contained values to prevent undefined behavior.
aou_req__result__value_handling.title: Result Value Handling

sections: Static Reflection with Serialization Component Architecture
comp__baselibs_static_reflection.title: Static Reflection with Serialization

comp_arc_dyn__baselibs__result.content: No need for sequence diagram. Simple caller callee flow.
comp_arc_dyn__baselibs__result.title: Result Dynamic view

sections: Static Reflection with Serialization Component Architecture
comp_arc_sta__baselibs__static_reflectio.title: Static Reflection with Serialization Static view

comp_req__bitmanipulation__bit_operation.content: The bit manipulation component shall provide an API for setting, clearing, toggling, and checking individual bits for any integral type up to 64 bits, returning boolean success status.
comp_req__bitmanipulation__bit_operation.title: Support for Bit Operations

comp_req__bitmanipulation__bitmask_opera.content: The bit manipulation library shall provide type-safe bitmask operations for scoped enumeration types.
comp_req__bitmanipulation__bitmask_opera.title: Support for Bitmask Operators for Enum Classes

comp_req__bitmanipulation__bounds_safety.content: The bit manipulation functions shall validate input parameters against bounds and, on out-of-bounds access, shall leave the target value unmodified and return false.
comp_req__bitmanipulation__bounds_safety.title: Bounds and Safety Checks

comp_req__bitmanipulation__byte_operatio.content: The bit manipulation component shall provide an API for extracting and setting bytes and half-bytes for any integral type up to 64 bits, returning boolean success status.
comp_req__bitmanipulation__byte_operatio.title: Support for Byte and Half-Byte Operations

comp_req__bitmanipulation__header_only.content: The bit manipulation API shall be header-only and not require external dependencies.
comp_req__bitmanipulation__header_only.title: Header-only API

comp_req__concurrency__condition_variabl.content: The concurrency module shall provide an abortable mechanism for threads to wait for conditions.
comp_req__concurrency__condition_variabl.title: Interruptible Condition Variable

comp_req__concurrency__delayed_task.content: The concurrency module shall provide implementation for executing callable objects after a specified delay with support for early cancellation.
comp_req__concurrency__delayed_task.title: Delayed Task Execution

comp_req__concurrency__error_handling.content: The concurrency module shall use error codes instead of throwing exceptions.
comp_req__concurrency__error_handling.title: Future Error Handling

comp_req__concurrency__executor_interfac.content: The concurrency module shall provide interface defining a common API for task submission operations, graceful shutdown, and maximum concurrency level reporting.
comp_req__concurrency__executor_interfac.title: Executor Interface

comp_req__concurrency__interruptible_wai.content: The concurrency module shall provide utilities offering APIs for sleep operations allowing early cancellation.
comp_req__concurrency__interruptible_wai.title: Interruptible Wait Utilities

comp_req__concurrency__long_running_thre.content: The concurrency module shall provide a container for managing long-running threads, allowing addition and removal of threads.
comp_req__concurrency__long_running_thre.title: Long-Running Threads Container

comp_req__concurrency__memory_reservatio.content: The concurrency module shall allow reserving memory upfront for tasks to ensure predictable resource allocation.
comp_req__concurrency__memory_reservatio.title: Memory Reservation

comp_req__concurrency__memory_usage_cont.content: The concurrency module shall limit memory usage to prevent uncontrolled resource consumption.
comp_req__concurrency__memory_usage_cont.title: Memory Usage Control

comp_req__concurrency__notification.content: The concurrency module shall allow one thread to notify another thread once, with a timeout option.
comp_req__concurrency__notification.title: Notification Mechanism

comp_req__concurrency__operation_timeout.content: The concurrency module shall avoid indefinitely blocking, unabortable operations.
comp_req__concurrency__operation_timeout.title: Operation Timeout Protection

comp_req__concurrency__periodic_task.content: The concurrency module shall provide implementation for executing callable objects at regular intervals with configurable first execution time and period duration.
comp_req__concurrency__periodic_task.title: Periodic Task Execution

comp_req__concurrency__simple_task.content: The concurrency module shall provide implementation for executing callable objects without returning results, supporting fire-and-forget asynchronous operations.
comp_req__concurrency__simple_task.title: Simple Task Implementation

comp_req__concurrency__synchronized_queu.content: The concurrency module shall provide a thread-safe container facilitating FIFO transmission of data in a N:1 communication pattern.
comp_req__concurrency__synchronized_queu.title: Synchronized Queue

comp_req__concurrency__task_cancellation.content: The concurrency module shall allow cancelling tasks when needed.
comp_req__concurrency__task_cancellation.title: Task Cancellation

comp_req__concurrency__task_interface.content: The concurrency module shall allow running tasks asynchronously.
comp_req__concurrency__task_interface.title: Asynchronous Task Execution

comp_req__concurrency__task_result.content: The concurrency module shall provide implementation to access the result of an asynchronously running task or to request aborting the task when the result is no longer needed.
comp_req__concurrency__task_result.title: Task Result Management

comp_req__concurrency__thread_count_repo.content: The concurrency module shall report the maximum number of concurrent threads available for task execution.
comp_req__concurrency__thread_count_repo.title: Thread Count Reporting

comp_req__concurrency__thread_pool.content: The concurrency module shall provide implementation that manages submitted tasks in FIFO order.
comp_req__concurrency__thread_pool.title: Thread Pool Implementation

comp_req__containers__deterministic_beha.content: The Containers library shall provide deterministic behavior with no dynamic memory allocation.
comp_req__containers__deterministic_beha.title: Deterministic Behavior

comp_req__containers__dynamic_array.content: The Containers library shall provide a fixed-size array container with construction-time size specification.
comp_req__containers__dynamic_array.title: Dynamic Array

comp_req__containers__intrusive_list.content: The Containers library shall provide an intrusive doubly-linked list based on the C++ standardization proposal P0406R1.
comp_req__containers__intrusive_list.title: Intrusive List

comp_req__containers__non_relocatable_ve.content: The Containers library shall provide a non-relocatable vector container that maintains stable element addresses.
comp_req__containers__non_relocatable_ve.title: Non-Relocatable Vector

comp_req__containers__type_safety.content: The Containers library shall enforce compile-time type safety for all container operations.
comp_req__containers__type_safety.title: Type Safety

comp_req__filesystem__api_abstraction.content: The Filesystem library shall provide filesystem API based on the C++ standardization.
comp_req__filesystem__api_abstraction.title: Standard Filesystem Abstraction

comp_req__filesystem__directory_iterator.content: The Filesystem library shall provide directory iterator types for traversing directory contents.
comp_req__filesystem__directory_iterator.title: Directory Iterators

comp_req__filesystem__file_io.content: The Filesystem library shall provide functionality for reading from and writing to files, including buffered I/O operations.
comp_req__filesystem__file_io.title: File I/O Operations

comp_req__filesystem__file_utils.content: The Filesystem library shall provide additional file utility functions, such as temporary file handling and file comparison.
comp_req__filesystem__file_utils.title: File Utility Functions

comp_req__filesystem__full_testability.content:
The public API of the library shall support dependency injection with test doubles.

Note: This enables full testability of the user code.
comp_req__filesystem__full_testability.title: Fully testable public API

comp_req__filesystem__mock_fake.content: The Filesystem library shall provide mock and fake implementations for unit testing and validation.
comp_req__filesystem__mock_fake.title: Mock and Fake Implementations

comp_req__filesystem__path_utilities.content: The Filesystem library shall provide type-safe utilities for path construction and manipulation.
comp_req__filesystem__path_utilities.title: Path Manipulation Utilities

comp_req__flatbuffers__access.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library shall provide functionality to read FlatBuffers binary data.

.. note::
   FlatBuffers uses a zero-copy approach where data is accessed directly from the binary buffer.
comp_req__flatbuffers__access.title: FlatBuffers Access

comp_req__flatbuffers__asil.content: The FlatBuffers library shall be ASIL-B compliant for C++ and Rust language support.
comp_req__flatbuffers__asil.title: FlatBuffers library ASIL level

comp_req__flatbuffers__buffer_identifica.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library shall provide a common opt-in buffer identification mechanism consisting
of a major version, a minor version, and a 4-character identifier.
comp_req__flatbuffers__buffer_identifica.title: Common Buffer Identification

comp_req__flatbuffers__serialization.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library shall provide functionality to serialize data into the FlatBuffers binary format.

.. note::
   Although this is only required for future use cases and not for the module configuration, it is already
   included, as excluding it would require extensive patching of ``flatc`` functionality.
comp_req__flatbuffers__serialization.title: FlatBuffers Serialization

comp_req__flatbuffers__verification.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library shall provide a verification mechanism to validate the structural well-formedness of a FlatBuffers buffer.

.. note::
   Verification only validates the buffer structure (e.g. offsets, vtables, field boundaries),
   not the correctness or integrity of the payload data.
comp_req__flatbuffers__verification.title: FlatBuffers Verification

comp_req__flatbuffers__version_check.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library shall provide a common opt-in version check mechanism that validates
the major version, minor version, and 4-character identifier of a FlatBuffers buffer.
comp_req__flatbuffers__version_check.title: Common Version Check

comp_req__json__asil.content: The JSON library shall be ASIL-B compliant.
comp_req__json__asil.title: JSON library ASIL level

comp_req__json__deserialization.content:
The JSON-Library shall provide a service to deserialize JSON data according to RFC8259, i.e.
parse and check for well-formedness.
comp_req__json__deserialization.title: JSON Deserialization

comp_req__json__full_testability.content:
The public API of the library shall support dependency injection with test doubles.

Note: This enables full testability of the user code.
comp_req__json__full_testability.section_name: Full testability for the user facing API
sections: Full testability for the user facing API
comp_req__json__full_testability.title: Fully testable public API

comp_req__json__lang_idioms.content: The public API shall support the idioms of the programming language it is written in.
comp_req__json__lang_idioms.section_name: User friendly API for information exchange
sections: User friendly API for information exchange
comp_req__json__lang_idioms.title: Support for programming language idioms

comp_req__json__lang_infra.content:
The public API shall use core infrastructure of its programming language and accompanying standard libraries,
whenever possible and meaningful.

Note: This includes error handling.
comp_req__json__lang_infra.section_name: User friendly API for information exchange
sections: User friendly API for information exchange
comp_req__json__lang_infra.title: Use programming language infrastructure

comp_req__json__serialization.content: The JSON-Library shall provide a service to serialize user format into JSON data according to RFC8259.
comp_req__json__serialization.title: JSON Serialization

comp_req__json__type_compatibility.content:
The public API shall enforce strict type compatibility. When a user requests a value, the API shall
validate that the requested return type is compatible with the type and value of the stored JSON data.

Note: This includes checking if the stored value exceeds the range of the expected type.
comp_req__json__type_compatibility.section_name: User friendly API for information exchange
sections: User friendly API for information exchange
comp_req__json__type_compatibility.title: Enforce strict type compatibility

comp_req__json__user_format.content:
The JSON-Library shall return parsed data in a compatible user format.

Note: The user format shall be a regular type and not defined within the library.
comp_req__json__user_format.title: Return data in user format

comp_req__memory__address_independence.content: The Memory library shall ensure shared memory data structures remain valid regardless of process virtual address space mappings.
comp_req__memory__address_independence.title: Process Address Space Independence

comp_req__memory__atomic_ops.content: The Memory library shall provide atomic operations on shared memory data for lock-free inter-process communication.
comp_req__memory__atomic_ops.title: Atomic Operations in Shared Memory

comp_req__memory__bounds_check.content: The Memory library shall track and validate memory region boundaries to prevent out-of-bounds access in shared memory.
comp_req__memory__bounds_check.title: Memory Region Bounds Checking

comp_req__memory__deterministic_alloc.content: The shared memory allocation shall provide deterministic behavior with predictable execution time suitable for real-time automotive systems.
comp_req__memory__deterministic_alloc.title: Deterministic Memory Allocation

comp_req__memory__endianness.content: The Memory library shall provide byte order conversion between host and network byte order (big/little endian).
comp_req__memory__endianness.title: Endianness Conversion

comp_req__memory__inter_process_sync.content: The Memory library shall provide file-based locking mechanisms for inter-process synchronization and mutual exclusion.
comp_req__memory__inter_process_sync.title: Inter-Process Synchronization

comp_req__memory__offset_ptr.content: The Memory library shall provide polymorphic memory resource allocators using offset pointers instead of raw pointers to enable allocation in shared memory regions accessible across multiple processes. The standard library's std::pmr::polymorphic_allocator is not applicable as it uses raw pointers that are invalid when shared memory is mapped at different virtual addresses in different processes.
comp_req__memory__offset_ptr.title: Polymorphic OffsetPtr Allocator

comp_req__memory__resource_registry.content: The Memory library shall provide a global registry for memory resource lookup and management.
comp_req__memory__resource_registry.title: Memory Resource Registry

comp_req__memory__sealed_shm.content: The Memory library shall provide immutable shared memory segments that become read-only after initialization.
comp_req__memory__sealed_shm.title: Sealed Shared Memory

comp_req__memory__shared_container.content: The Memory library shall provide type aliases for STL containers (vector, map, string) that use offset pointers for shared memory storage.
comp_req__memory__shared_container.title: Shared Memory Container

comp_req__memory__shared_memory.content: The Memory library shall provide capabilities for creating, opening and managing shared memory.
comp_req__memory__shared_memory.title: Shared Memory Management

comp_req__memory__string_utils.content: The Memory library shall provide zero-allocation string utilities including splitting, comparison, and compile-time literals.
comp_req__memory__string_utils.title: String Utilities

comp_req__memory__typed_memory.content: The Memory library shall provide an interface to allocate shared memory from typed memory regions.
comp_req__memory__typed_memory.title: Typed Memory

comp_req__result__deterministic_behavior.content: The Result library shall provide deterministic behavior with no dynamic memory allocation.
comp_req__result__deterministic_behavior.title: Deterministic Behavior

comp_req__result__domain_error_informati.content: The Result library shall support user-defined error domains and error codes.
comp_req__result__domain_error_informati.title: Domain-Specific Error Information

comp_req__result__error_handling.content: The Result library shall provide an error handling mechanism that enables functions to return either successful values ("Get Value") or error information ("Get Error") without using C++ exceptions.
comp_req__result__error_handling.title: Result-Based Error Handling

comp_req__result__exception_free_operati.content: The Result library shall operate without throwing C++ exceptions.
comp_req__result__exception_free_operati.title: Exception-Free Operation

comp_req__result__set_result.content: The Result library shall provide a "Set Result" operation that accepts either a successful value of type T or an Error object.
comp_req__result__set_result.title: Set Result

comp_req__result__std_integration.content: The Result library shall provide conversion utilities to transform Result objects into std::optional<T> types. The conversion shall require explicit handling of error cases before discarding error information, ensuring that errors cannot be silently ignored.
comp_req__result__std_integration.title: Standard Library Integration

comp_req__result__type_safety.content: The Result library shall enforce compile-time type safety for error handling operations.
comp_req__result__type_safety.title: Type-Safe Error Handling

comp_req__safecpp__aborts_upon_exception.content:
The safecpp library shall provide a mechanism to abort the program upon C++ exception allocation,
allowing users to prevent any exception object from being created and hence to guarantee that
no single exception will be possible to be thrown within an executable.

.. Note::
      This satisfies :need:`aou_req__platform__no_exceptions`
comp_req__safecpp__aborts_upon_exception.title: C++ Exception Abort

comp_req__safecpp__coverage_termination.content:
The safecpp library shall provide a mechanism which automatically registers a termination handler
which ensures that code coverage data is also persisted upon any process exit (e.g. during gtest's Death Tests).
comp_req__safecpp__coverage_termination.title: Code Coverage Termination

comp_req__safecpp__nullstring.content:
The safecpp library shall provide a view type which guarantees that an underlying character sequence is guaranteed to be null-terminated.
Such views type's underlying character sequence shall not be possible to get modified via such view type's methods.
comp_req__safecpp__nullstring.title: Null-Terminated String

comp_req__safecpp__safe_atomic.content: The safecpp library shall provide a lock-free atomic addition operation for integral types with overflow detection.
comp_req__safecpp__safe_atomic.title: Safe Atomic operations

comp_req__safecpp__safe_math.content:
The safecpp library shall provide arithmetic operations
(addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, negation, absolute value, type casting, and comparison operations)
that detect overflow, underflow, divide-by-zero, and precision loss for both integer and floating-point types,
ensuring errors are handled safely and undefined behavior is prevented.
comp_req__safecpp__safe_math.title: Safe Arithmetic Operations

comp_req__safecpp__scoped_guards.content: The safecpp library shall provide scope-bound callable wrappers that prevent resource leaks.
comp_req__safecpp__scoped_guards.title: Scoped Guards

comp_req__static_reflect_serial__compile.content: The library shall provide compile-time safety and efficiency of serialization operations with minimal runtime overhead.
comp_req__static_reflect_serial__compile.title: Compile-Time Efficiency

comp_req__static_reflect_serial__contain.content: The library shall automatically traverse containers using appropriate iteration.
comp_req__static_reflect_serial__contain.title: Automatic Container Iteration

comp_req__static_reflect_serial__header_.content: The library shall be implemented as a header-only library to enable compile-time optimizations and avoid runtime dependencies.
comp_req__static_reflect_serial__header_.title: Header-Only Implementation

comp_req__static_reflect_serial__nested.content: The library shall support serialization and visitation of nested data structures.
comp_req__static_reflect_serial__nested.title: Nested Type Support

comp_req__static_reflect_serial__reflect.content: The library shall provide compile-time reflection-based serialization and deserialization for C++ Data Structures.
comp_req__static_reflect_serial__reflect.title: Static Reflection Support

comp_req__static_reflect_serial__visitor.content: The library shall provide a visitor pattern for traversal of C++ data structures.
comp_req__static_reflect_serial__visitor.title: Generic Visitor Pattern

comp_req__utils__base64.content: The Utils component shall provide functions for encoding data to Base64 format and decoding Base64 data back to its original form.
comp_req__utils__base64.title: Base64 Encoding and Decoding

comp_req__utils__deterministic_behavior.content: The Utils component shall ensure that all operations complete in a predictable manner and without dynamic memory allocation.
comp_req__utils__deterministic_behavior.title: Deterministic Behavior

comp_req__utils__scoped_operation.content: The Utils component shall provide a functionality that stores a callback and executes it automatically when the class is destructed
comp_req__utils__scoped_operation.title: Scoped Operation Management

comp_req__vajson__asil.content: The VaJson library shall be ASIL-B compliant.
comp_req__vajson__asil.title: VaJson library ASIL level

comp_req__vajson__hex_integers.content:
VaJson shall accept hexadecimal integers.
This behavior extends RFC 8259.
comp_req__vajson__hex_integers.title: JSON Deserialization RFC extension hexadecimal integers

comp_req__vajson__trailing_commas.content:
VaJson shall ignore trailing commas.
This behavior extends RFC 8259.
comp_req__vajson__trailing_commas.title: JSON Deserialization RFC extension trailing commas

comp_req__vajson__unicode.content: VaJson shall encode and decode UTF-8 encoded strings.
comp_req__vajson__unicode.title: Unicode Support

comp_req__vajson__validation.content:
VaJson checks the well-formedness of JSON data.
Errors shall include the reason and location in the JSON document for malformed JSON.
comp_req__vajson__validation.title: JSON Validation

comp_saf_fmea__result__error_code.content:
If the user relies on the error code not only for diagnostic purposes but for selecting an error reaction path,
misinterpreting the code under a wrong domain could lead to an incorrect safety-relevant decision.
An Assumption of Use shall ensure the user verifies the error domain before interpreting the error code.
comp_saf_fmea__result__error_code.failure_effect: When retrieving error information, the error code is returned as a domain-agnostic integer. If the user interprets this code under a different error domain than the one that produced it, the error is misidentified, potentially leading to incorrect error reaction.
comp_saf_fmea__result__error_code.title: Result Error Code Cross-Domain Misinterpretation
comp_saf_fmea__result__error_code.type_name: Component FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)

comp_saf_fmea__result__error_message_lif.content:
The existing Assumption of Use for resource lifetime addresses the validity of error domain objects
and referenced resources. However, it does not explicitly cover the user-provided error message,
which is equally subject to lifetime constraints. The AoU should be extended to explicitly include the error message data,
or a separate AoU should be established for it.
comp_saf_fmea__result__error_message_lif.failure_effect: The error message provided during error construction is stored as a non-owning reference. If the referenced data is no longer valid when the user retrieves the error message, accessing it results in undefined behavior.
comp_saf_fmea__result__error_message_lif.title: Result Error Message Lifetime Violation
comp_saf_fmea__result__error_message_lif.type_name: Component FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)

comp_saf_fmea__result__stop_user.content:
The Result library invokes user-provided operations synchronously during transformation of values or errors.
Ensuring these operations terminate is outside the scope of the library and is the responsibility of the user via program flow monitoring,
as covered by the referenced platform-level Assumption of Use.
comp_saf_fmea__result__stop_user.failure_effect: The user provides a transformation or error handling operation to the Result library. If this operation does not terminate (e.g., infinite loop), the calling execution is blocked indefinitely.
comp_saf_fmea__result__stop_user.title: Result Stop User
comp_saf_fmea__result__stop_user.type_name: Component FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)

comp_saf_fmea__result__unchecked.content:
If the user accesses the value or the error without first verifying the state of the result,
the program will deterministically terminate. The provided Assumptions of Use require the user to check and handle both states before access.
comp_saf_fmea__result__unchecked.failure_effect: If the user calls value without the result containing a value, or calls error without the result containing an error, the program will terminate. This may occur when the user does not check the state of the result before accessing it.
comp_saf_fmea__result__unchecked.title: Result Unchecked Value or Error Access
comp_saf_fmea__result__unchecked.type_name: Component FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)

doc__static_refl_with_serial_arch.section_name: Static Reflection with Serialization Component Architecture
sections: Static Reflection with Serialization Component Architecture
doc__static_refl_with_serial_arch.title: static_reflection_with_serialization Architecture

doc__static_reflect_serial_lib_req.title: Static Reflection with Serialization Library Requirements

feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__blocked_executio.content:
All the baselibs components have to be developed to ASIL_B standard to maintain Freedom From Interference,
all blocks should be detected by unit testing.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__blocked_executio.failure_effect: Using application is blocked from execution and thus cannot fulfill its safety function
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__blocked_executio.title: blocked execution

feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__conc_file_access.content: The user has to care for concurrent file access. This is not covered by the filesytem library.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__conc_file_access.failure_effect: Concurrent file access may lead to corruption of the file
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__conc_file_access.title: concurrent file access

feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__conc_memory_acce.content: The user has to care for concurrent memory access. This is not covered by the bitmanipulation library.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__conc_memory_acce.failure_effect: Concurrent memory access may lead to corruption of the memory
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__conc_memory_acce.title: concurrent memory access

feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__cpu_starvation.content:
Some care is taken to avoid using too much CPU time, but this cannot be covered fully.
Platform level AoU asks applications with timing requirements to cover this by program flow monitoring.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__cpu_starvation.failure_effect: CPU starvation leads to delayed execution and may violate safety timing requirements.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__cpu_starvation.title: CPU starvation

feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__locked_ressource.content:
Only components "filesystem" and "memory_shared" should have the problem ("bitmanipulation" should not be affected due to shortness of execution)
"memory_shared" cares for this by above linked feature requirement and :need:`comp_req__memory__atomic_ops`.
"filesystem" component may fail on this but this is covered by common platform aou linked above.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__locked_ressource.failure_effect: Deadlock/Livelock leads to stalling of the execution
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__locked_ressource.title: locked ressource

feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__memory_access.content:
All the baselibs components have to be developed to ASIL_B standard to maintain Freedom From Interference,
out of bounds access should be detected by unit testing/sanitizers.
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__memory_access.failure_effect: memory of using component may be corrupted leading to safety requirement violation
feat_saf_dfa__baselibs__memory_access.title: memory access

tool_req__flatbuffers_codegen_cpp.content: The FlatBuffers-Library tooling shall generate code for serialization and read access of FlatBuffers data for C++.
tool_req__flatbuffers_codegen_cpp.title: FlatBuffers Code Generation for C++

tool_req__flatbuffers_codegen_python.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library tooling shall generate code for serialization and read access of FlatBuffers data for Python.

.. note::
   Python code generation is nice-to-have for benchmark testing (scale configurations).
tool_req__flatbuffers_codegen_python.title: FlatBuffers Code Generation for Python

tool_req__flatbuffers_codegen_rust.content: The FlatBuffers-Library tooling shall generate code for serialization and read access of FlatBuffers data for Rust.
tool_req__flatbuffers_codegen_rust.title: FlatBuffers Code Generation for Rust

tool_req__flatbuffers_tooling_data_valid.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library tooling shall provide a mechanism to validate JSON-encoded files containing
FlatBuffers data against JSON-schema defined semantic constraints such as value ranges, allowed values
and required field presence, in case of violation of the JSON-schema the tool shall fail with an appropriate error message.

.. note::
   FlatBuffers schemas constrain values only via type bounds (e.g. uint8) or enum membership, arbitrary
   constraints such as custom value ranges must be enforced externally.
   Within FlatBuffers tables, scalar fields always carry a value (their default if not explicitly set)
   and cannot be absent. Reference-type fields (strings, vectors, nested tables, unions) can be
   marked with required to enforce their presence, otherwise they are optional.
tool_req__flatbuffers_tooling_data_valid.title: FlatBuffers Data Constraint Validation

tool_req__flatbuffers_tooling_evolution.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library tooling shall provide a mechanism to check whether a new version of a
FlatBuffers schema is backward compatible with a previous version.

.. note::
   Backward compatibility in FlatBuffers requires that existing fields are not removed or
   reordered, field types are not changed, and deprecated fields retain their field identifier.
   Breaking these rules silently corrupts data when old binaries access buffers produced from
   a new schema or vice versa.
tool_req__flatbuffers_tooling_evolution.title: FlatBuffers Schema Evolution Check

tool_req__flatbuffers_tooling_json_to_bi.content:
The FlatBuffers-Library tooling shall support creation of FlatBuffers binary files from
JSON-encoded files conforming to the provided FlatBuffers schema, in case of violation
of the FlatBuffers schema the tool shall fail with an appropriate error message.
tool_req__flatbuffers_tooling_json_to_bi.title: FlatBuffers Binary Creation from JSON

arch.description: Mapping of uml key to uml content.

constraints.description: List of constraint names, which are defined for this need.

constraints_error.description: An error message set if any constraint failed, and `error_message` field is set in config.

constraints_passed.description: True if all constraints passed, False if any failed, None if not yet checked.

constraints_results.description: Mapping of constraint name, to check name, to result, None if not yet checked.

content.description: The main content of the need.

docname.description: Name of the document where the need is defined (None if external).

doctype.description: The markup type of the content, denoted by the suffix of the source file, e.g. '.rst'.

external_css.description: CSS class name, added to the external reference.

external_url.description: URL of the need, if it is an external need.

has_dead_links.description: True if any links reference need ids that are not found in the need list.

has_forbidden_dead_links.description: True if any links reference need ids that are not found in the need list, and the link type does not allow dead links.

is_external.description: If true, no node is created and need is referencing external url.

is_import.description: If true, the need was derived from an import.

is_modified.description: Whether the need was modified by needextend.

jinja_content.description: Whether the content was pre-processed by jinja.

layout.description: Key of the layout, which is used to render the need.

lineno.description: Line number where the need is defined (None if external).

parts.description: Mapping of parts, a.k.a. sub-needs, IDs to data that overrides the need's data

post_template.description: The template key, if the post_content was created from a jinja template.

pre_template.description: The template key, if the pre_content was created from a jinja template.

signature.description: Derived from a docutils desc_name node.

style.description: Comma-separated list of CSS classes (all appended by `needs_style_`).

template.description: The template key, if the content was created from a jinja template.
